gasly.personal: Pierre Gasly was born in Rouen, France, into a family with deep roots in motorsport; his grandfather, father, and brothers all competed in karting or racing. He began karting at age six and eventually moved to Le Mans as a teenager to pursue a professional career within the French driving school system. Outside of racing, Gasly is a devoted supporter of Paris Saint-Germain football club and maintains a keen interest in high fashion and music. He is also widely known for his close friendship with the late Anthoine Hubert, which has remained a central emotional touchstone throughout his life and career.
gasly.career: Gasly’s ascent to Formula 1 was highlighted by his 2016 GP2 Series title and a successful stint in Japan's Super Formula before debuting with Toro Rosso in late 2017. His career is most famous for a resilient comeback; after being demoted from Red Bull Racing mid-way through 2019, he secured an emotional maiden victory at the 2020 Italian Grand Prix with AlphaTauri, becoming France's first winner in 24 years. Having moved to the Alpine factory team in 2023, Gasly has continued to build his legacy as a consistent podium finisher and a key leader for the French national project in F1.

arvid_lindblad.personal: Arvid Lindblad was born on August 8, 2007, in London, England, and possesses dual British and Swedish citizenship, though he competes professionally under the British banner. Growing up in a household where his father, Peter, encouraged his competitive drive, Lindblad began karting at age five and quickly developed a reputation for clinical speed and tactical awareness. Beyond the racetrack, the young driver is a dedicated student who enjoys staying active through various sports, maintaining a high level of physical fitness and mental conditioning to balance the intense pressure of being a high-profile athlete within the Red Bull program.
arvid_lindblad.career: Managed by the Red Bull Junior Team since 2021, Lindblad has enjoyed a rapid progression through the motorsport ladder, highlighted by championship success in high-level karting before moving to single-seaters. His career gained significant momentum with Prema Racing in the Italian F4 Championship and a victory at the 2023 Macau Formula 4 Grand Prix, which served as a springboard for his promotion to FIA Formula 3. In 2024, he emerged as a standout talent in his debut F3 season with Prema, securing multiple race victories and solidifying his trajectory as one of the sport’s most anticipated prospects currently preparing for an eventual promotion to the Formula 1 grid.

alonso.personal: Born in Oviedo, Spain, in 1981, Fernando Alonso’s racing journey began when his father, an amateur kart enthusiast, built a kart intended for his older sister that Fernando inherited at age three. Growing up in a modest household, his early talent was nurtured by his family's dedication, leading him to several national titles and a world karting championship by his teens. Outside the cockpit, the Spaniard is a devoted fan of Real Madrid and an avid cyclist, often noted for his intense competitive nature and a long-standing personal hobby of performing magic and card tricks.
alonso.career: Alonso debuted in Formula 1 with Minardi in 2001 before joining Renault, where he famously ended Michael Schumacher’s era of dominance by securing back-to-back World Championships in 2005 and 2006. Throughout a storied career featuring high-profile stints at McLaren, Ferrari, and Alpine, he established himself as one of the grid's most versatile and relentless operators, amassing 32 victories and over 100 podiums. Currently competing for Aston Martin, his longevity is unprecedented, marked by his pursuit of the Triple Crown of Motorsport and his record for the most race starts in the history of the sport.

colapinto.personal: Franco Colapinto was born on May 27, 2003, in Pilar, Buenos Aires, and stands as the first Argentine driver to compete in Formula 1 in over two decades. His passion for speed was influenced by his father, Aníbal, who was a competitor and team owner in Argentina’s domestic racing scene. At just 14 years old, Franco moved to Europe alone to live in a karting factory in Italy, a sacrifice that defined his early career. Beyond the track, he is a fervent supporter of the Boca Juniors football club and has become a viral sensation for his charismatic personality and unfiltered wit in media interviews.
colapinto.career: Colapinto’s ascent through the junior categories was fueled by a dominant title in the 2019 Spanish F4 Championship, followed by race-winning campaigns in FIA Formula 3 and Formula 2 with MP Motorsport. As a standout member of the Williams Racing Driver Academy, he was unexpectedly promoted mid-season to replace Logan Sargeant at Williams for the 2024 Italian Grand Prix. He reached a historic milestone at the 2024 Azerbaijan Grand Prix, finishing eighth to become the first Argentine to score Formula 1 points since Carlos Reutemann in 1982, quickly cementing his reputation as a formidable talent on the grid.

hulkenberg.personal: Born in Emmerich am Rhein, West Germany, in 1987, Nico Hülkenberg began karting at age ten, quickly demonstrating the clinical precision that would define his professional style. Unlike many of his peers from deep-rooted racing dynasties, Hülkenberg’s path was forged through the logistical support of his father, Klaus, who operated a shipping company. Outside the cockpit, the polyglot athlete—fluent in German, Dutch, and English—is an avid fan of tennis and cycling, often seen training near his home in Monaco where he resides with his wife, Egle, and their daughter. He is affectionately known as 'The Hulk,' a moniker reflecting both his name and his formidable physical presence on the grid.
hulkenberg.career: Hülkenberg’s ascent to Formula 1 was meteoric, marked by dominant titles in Formula BMW, A1GP, and the GP2 Series, where he clinched the championship as a rookie in 2009. He made his F1 debut with Williams in 2010, famously securing a surprise pole position at the Brazilian Grand Prix during his maiden season. Over a career spanning tenures at Force India, Sauber, Renault, and Haas, he has earned a reputation as one of the grid’s most reliable and technically gifted drivers, frequently called upon as a 'super-sub' during the COVID-19 era. While he notably holds the record for the most race starts without a podium, his 2015 victory at the 24 Hours of Le Mans with Porsche remains a crowning achievement that highlights his elite versatility.

stroll.personal: Lance Stroll was born on October 29, 1998, in Montreal, Canada, to billionaire businessman Lawrence Stroll and fashion designer Claire-Anne Callens. Raised in an environment of significant wealth and sporting interest, he became an accomplished athlete outside of racing, particularly in ice hockey where he remains a devoted fan of the Montreal Canadiens. His dual Canadian-Belgian heritage and his family’s prominent role in the fashion world shaped a private yet high-profile upbringing focused on international competition and diverse athletic pursuits like surfing and skiing.
stroll.career: Stroll entered Formula 1 in 2017 with Williams after clinching the Italian F4 and FIA European Formula 3 titles in quick succession. During his debut season, he became the youngest rookie to secure a podium finish with a standout drive to third place at the Azerbaijan Grand Prix. His career evolved through the transition of his team into Racing Point and eventually Aston Martin, where he claimed a stunning maiden pole position at the 2020 Turkish Grand Prix and added two more podiums. Known for his exceptional performance in wet-weather conditions, Stroll has established himself as a durable presence on the grid through various team iterations.

perez.personal: Born in Guadalajara, Mexico, Sergio Pérez is the youngest child of Antonio Pérez Garibay, a former racing driver and politician, and Marilú Mendoza. His older brother, Antonio, also pursued a professional racing career in NASCAR Mexico, fostering a family legacy that deeply influenced Sergio’s early entry into the sport. Outside of his professional life, Pérez is a passionate fan of the Club América football team and a devout Catholic, often dedicating his time to the Checo Pérez Foundation, which provides support and resources to orphans and children with cancer.
perez.career: Pérez made his Formula 1 debut with Sauber in 2011, quickly earning a reputation as a 'tire whisperer' due to his exceptional ability to manage rubber over long race stints. After a challenging year at McLaren in 2013, he spent seven seasons with the Force India and Racing Point outfit, where he secured numerous podiums and a historic maiden win at the 2020 Sakhir Grand Prix. Since joining Red Bull Racing in 2021, he has become the most successful Mexican driver in history, achieving multiple wins on prestigious street circuits like Monaco and Baku, and finishing as the World Championship runner-up in 2023.

piastri.personal: Born in Melbourne, Australia, in 2001, Oscar Piastri is of Italian-Australian heritage and developed his passion for racing through remote-controlled cars before moving to karts. His father, Chris, played a pivotal role in his early development, serving as his mechanic during the formative years of his competitive career. Away from the circuit, Piastri is noted for his dry wit and calm temperament, and he remains a dedicated follower of cricket and the Australian Football League, specifically supporting the Richmond Tigers.
piastri.career: Piastri reached Formula 1 following a historic run in the junior categories, where he became the first driver to win the Formula Renault Eurocup, FIA Formula 3, and FIA Formula 2 championships in successive rookie seasons. After a high-profile legal dispute regarding his contract between Alpine and McLaren, he debuted for the Woking-based team in 2023. Since then, he has established himself as a frontrunner, claiming a Sprint race victory in Qatar during his debut year and securing his maiden Grand Prix win at the 2024 Hungarian Grand Prix.

bearman.personal: Oliver Bearman, born in May 2005 in Chelmsford, Essex, is a prominent British talent whose ascent through the motorsport ranks was fueled by a childhood obsession with speed rather than a professional racing lineage. Supported closely by his father David, Bearman moved to Italy as a teenager to join the Ferrari Driver Academy, a pivotal step in his international career. When not behind the wheel, he is an avid golfer and maintains a rigorous physical training schedule, often sharing his journey with a supportive family circle that remains central to his life.
bearman.career: Bearman's journey to the pinnacle of motorsport is marked by a historic 2021 season where he became the first driver to win both the ADAC and Italian F4 titles simultaneously. After a strong showing in Formula 3 and several victories in Formula 2 with Prema Racing, he made a sensational Formula 1 debut for Scuderia Ferrari at the 2024 Saudi Arabian Grand Prix, finishing seventh. This performance made him the youngest British driver in history and earned him a 2025 full-time seat with the Haas F1 Team, for whom he also competed as a substitute during the 2024 season.

norris.personal: Born in Bristol in 1999, Lando Norris is a dual British-Belgian national who discovered his passion for motorsport at age seven after watching a local karting event. He is the son of Adam Norris, a highly successful retired pensions manager who supported a junior career that saw Lando become the youngest-ever CIK-FIA World Champion in the KF class. Outside the cockpit, Norris is a high-profile content creator and founder of the gaming and apparel brand Quadrant, and he is frequently praised for his transparency regarding mental health and his enthusiasm for golf and photography.
norris.career: Norris's path to the pinnacle of motorsport was marked by immediate success, including title wins in the MSA Formula, Toyota Racing Series, and the FIA Formula 3 European Championship. He made his Formula 1 debut with McLaren in 2019, quickly establishing a reputation for exceptional qualifying pace and race craft. After several years of consistent podium finishes and a near-miss in Sochi 2021, he secured his maiden victory at the 2024 Miami Grand Prix and went on to claim two more wins in the Netherlands and Singapore, ultimately finishing the 2024 season as the championship runner-up.
